name: Hero scene
purpose: Full-bleed homepage hero. Painterly Pixar-style studio with Bernese mountain dog, Golden Gate Bridge, cork board, sketchbook, monitor glow.
where_deployed: Top of index.html (lines 147-152)
style_direction: Pixar painterly, warm golden hour, tilt-shift quality

name: Favicon — favicon.ico
purpose: Multi-resolution browser tab icon (16, 32, 48px). Shows the Fenix mascot character.
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30. Old M-logo files preserved in images/favicon/_kg-logo-backup/ for revert if needed.

name: Favicon — favicon.svg
purpose: Modern browser pinned tab support. Currently a PNG-embedded SVG wrapper of the Fenix mascot.
source_method: Programmatic (PIL — base64-embedded 192px PNG inside SVG wrapper)
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30. True vector version would require an SVG-native illustration of the Fenix character — not currently available.

name: Favicon — favicon-96x96.png
purpose: 96×96 PNG favicon — shows the Fenix mascot character
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30.

name: Apple touch icon
purpose: iOS home screen icon when site is added to home screen. Shows the Fenix mascot character.
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30.

name: Web app manifest 192
purpose: PWA install icon (192×192) — Fenix mascot character
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30.

name: Web app manifest 512
purpose: PWA install icon (512×512) — Fenix mascot character
notes: Replaced the Kiran-Rao M-logo on 2026-04-30.

name: site.webmanifest
purpose: JSON PWA manifest — name, icons, theme color, install behavior
notes: Manifest structure unchanged in the 2026-04-30 favicon swap — only the underlying icon files updated. Theme color stays #0a0a0a (dark).

name: Fenix favicon source crop
purpose: 1024×1024 cropped source image used to generate the favicon set programmatically. Full-body Fenix character.
source_method: Cropped from images/fenix/1fenixavatar1.png via scripts/generate-fenix-favicons (PIL crop + LANCZOS resize)
notes: Don't delete — required to regenerate the favicon set if needed. Could become source for future watermark, sticker, or merch assets.

name: Logo / wordmark
purpose: Primary logo on site nav. Future state: SVG variant + monochrome variants needed.
notes: Could use SVG conversion + monochrome variants for stationery/print.

name: Bento card art (full system)
purpose: Per-persona-per-slot character illustrations across 9 cards × 7 personas. 23 unique images covering all 63 combos.
where_deployed: Homepage bento grid
style_direction: Pixar / Monsters Inc quality. Warm golden-hour. Per-character thematic environments. Compose for glassmorphism overlay dead-zone.

name: Persona portraits (set of 6)
purpose: Full-body character portraits for persona picker selection screen.
where_deployed: Persona picker (homepage first load)
notes: Files: evaluator-merritt, seeker-phil, practitioner-drew, learner-paige, technologist-ray, innercircle-keshav

name: OG share cards (9 per content type)
purpose: Per-content-type social link previews. 1200×630 (1.91:1).
where_deployed: og:image meta tag on every site page
style_direction: Bento hero image as background + thin cream bottom band (120px) with eyebrow + title + byline. Generated programmatically.

name: OG share card — Fenix
purpose: Fenix-character OG card available for any Fenix-related page. Sits alongside the per-content-type OG cards.
where_deployed: Available at images/og/og-fenix.png — wired on demand for any Fenix-related page (e.g., a future /fenix landing page). No page references it yet.
style_direction: Three-quarter body Fenix mascot. Right third of frame leaves space for headline typography. NOT generated like the other OG cards (no bottom band) — uses the full Fenix character as the hero.
source_method: Copied from images/fenix/2fenixhero1.png on 2026-04-30 (the OG hero variant of the Fenix mascot).
notes: Pairs with the Fenix mascot system. If wiring to a /fenix or /about-fenix page later, set its og:image to /images/og/og-fenix.png.

name: 404 / error page monster (RETIRED)
purpose: Construction-monster Pixar character originally used as the shared error-page hero across 404, 500, 503, maintenance.
where_deployed: RETIRED on 2026-04-30 — replaced by the Fenix mascot (sign-holder for 404/500/503, regeneration variant for maintenance). File still present on disk in case revert is needed.
style_direction: Construction-monster Pixar character, hard hat + tool belt + blueprint, warm golden lighting.
notes: Marked retired — file is shipped/exists but no production code references it. Safe to delete from disk if Fenix error system holds up over time.

name: Fenix mascot — avatar
purpose: Calm canonical Fenix portrait, head and chest. Used as chat widget avatar, favicon-mode, small thumbnail across CC and product surfaces.
where_deployed: index.html line 374 — 'Meet Fenix' intro logo (replaced images/logo.png). Future: chat widget avatar, alternate favicon (Fenix mode), CC dashboard Fenix tile.
style_direction: Pixar character animation cartoon mascot phoenix with raptor anatomy. Oversized expressive head, big warm friendly eyes, hooked eagle beak, prominent character brow. Subtle flame plumes on crest, gentle embers. Yellow chest, amber shoulders, crimson wingtips. Cream background.
notes: Calm canonical of the two-canonical Fenix system. Pair with maintenance regeneration (full-fire variant). Used as --sref anchor for downstream variants.

name: Fenix mascot — OG hero
purpose: Three-quarter body Fenix for social link previews on Fenix-related pages.
where_deployed: Available as og:image at images/og/og-fenix.png — wired on demand for any Fenix-related page
style_direction: Same Fenix character. Three-quarter body, relaxed alert pose. Right third of frame reserved for headline typography overlay.
notes: Source file lives in images/fenix/. Copied to images/og/og-fenix.png to match the per-content-type OG card naming pattern — that path is what gets referenced in og:image meta tags.

name: Fenix mascot — bento card hero
purpose: Workshop-studio scene of Fenix at work. Wired as the hero image on the site's password gate (under-construction overlay) — the first thing every visitor sees.
where_deployed: gate.js — site access gate (password overlay shown before any page until visitor enters workshop2026). Future: also slated for a homepage Fenix bento slot when the bento gets redesigned.
style_direction: Same Fenix character in a softly-lit warm studio environment, perched beside a glowing screen suggesting AI-assistant-at-work. Bottom-left dead zone for overlay.
notes: Originally seeded for a homepage bento Fenix slot (currently no slot exists). Repurposed as the gate hero — the workshop scene matches the 'site being built' message exactly.

name: Fenix mascot — error sign-holder
purpose: Apologetic Fenix holding a blank sign — the friendly error illustration for 404 / 500 / 503. Replaced the construction monster (images/404image.png).
where_deployed: error-styles.css default .error-hero — applied across 404.html, 500.html, 503.html
style_direction: Same Fenix character standing sheepishly, holding blank wooden / paper sign in front of chest with one wing. Body language reads 'oops, sorry about that' — head tilted, free wing in shrug or back-of-head scratch.
notes: Sign is intentionally blank in source asset — text gets layered in via CSS or per-page render so each error code can show different copy.

name: Fenix mascot — maintenance regeneration
purpose: Fenix mid-rebirth, body fully engulfed in active flames. Used on the maintenance / lockdown screen — the phoenix metaphor IS the maintenance metaphor. Copy reads 'Fenix is regenerating. Back shortly.'
where_deployed: maintenance.html — applied via body.fenix-regenerating override in error-styles.css
style_direction: Same character but FULL-fire variant — entire body engulfed in active warm flames, rising calmly from soft glowing embers, head held up serenely, ONE wing softly raised. Inviting fire, not threatening — golden ember light, drifting sparks, no smoke.
source_method: Midjourney v7 (sref to flame canonical, sw 300)
notes: Flame canonical of the two-canonical Fenix system. Filename has typo (maintainance vs maintenance) — keep as-is for now to avoid breaking links.

name: Fenix expression reference sheet
purpose: Multi-pose model sheet of Fenix in different emotional states (alert, thinking, sleeping, surprised, celebrating). Source for reaction images, blog post inserts, social posts, future content.
where_deployed: Not yet wired — kept as a content library for pulling specific expressions when needed.
style_direction: Same Fenix character at consistent pose, head/upper-body framing, repeated 5 times across the canvas in different expressions. Cream background, even lighting.
source_method: Midjourney v7 (sref to canonical avatar, sw 250)
notes: Single MJ generation gives 5 variants in one image. Canva-crop into 5 individual avatar tiles after.

name: Fenix sticker pack
purpose: Vinyl sticker pack — 4 Fenix character poses for conferences, swag, fans. Replaces the generic 'Sticker sheet' as the Fenix-specific variant.
where_deployed: Print-ready PDF assembled in Canva from 4 individual MJ generations. Used for conference giveaways and brand-kit merch.
style_direction: Same Fenix character. 4 distinct poses: (1) confident standing greeting, (2) thinking with wing on chin, (3) celebrating with wings up, (4) sleeping curled up. Cream background. Each generation cropped tight to the character with die-cut margin.
source_method: Midjourney v7 (sref to canonical avatar, sw 250) + Canva (die-cut layout)
notes: 4 separate MJ generations. Canva combines into a sticker sheet PDF for printing.

name: Fenix watermark mark
purpose: Single-color flat Fenix silhouette suitable for watermarking artwork or branding documents. Mid-century editorial treatment, NOT painterly.
where_deployed: Not yet wired — available for stationery, document footers, image watermarks, brand-doc embeds.
style_direction: Mid-century editorial flat geometric silhouette of the Fenix character. Single bold rust or charcoal color on cream background. Saul Bass / Lufthansa crane sensibility — iconic, heraldic, scales from 16px to billboard. NOT painterly Pixar — this is the brand-mark treatment of the character.
source_method: Midjourney v7 (separate sref — Saul Bass style, NOT canonical Fenix sref) + manual SVG conversion in Figma/Illustrator
notes: Different aesthetic from the Pixar Fenix character — this is the brand-mark version. The two co-exist (Pixar Fenix on product surfaces, mid-century mark on stationery + brand docs).

name: Fenix t-shirt graphic
purpose: Front-of-shirt Fenix character graphic, screen-print compatible. Limited palette, high-contrast, simplified shapes.
where_deployed: Brand-kit merch — t-shirt giveaway / event swag.
style_direction: Same Fenix character but simplified for screen-print: limited 3-color palette (yellow, amber, rust), strong outline, flat color fields, no painterly shading or gradients. DTG or screen-print compatible. Optional 'KIRANRAO.AI' wordmark below the character or single bold tagline.
source_method: Midjourney v7 (sref to canonical avatar, sw 200) + manual cleanup for print
notes: Pairs with Fenix tote bag and sticker pack for cohesive merch line.

name: Fenix mascot — manifesto opening frame
purpose: Opening frame source for the manifesto video. Fenix walking into frame from the right, looking at camera, ready to speak.
where_deployed: index.html manifesto section (C10) — wired as background-image poster on .manifesto-placeholder in styles.css. Also slated as source frame for videos/manifesto.mp4 once that lands.
style_direction: Same Fenix character entering frame from right, walking calmly forward, looking directly at camera, body language of a character about to speak. Soft warm depth-of-field studio behind. Negative space on right for first text title to fade in.
notes: Source frame for video. Animation/motion treatment is a separate workstream — this is the still that anchors it.

where_deployed: linkedin.com/in/[handle] header
